cadence: Business days, noon and 4-5 p.m. CT
notes: Best statewide Minnesota source. The portal says data is updated periodically throughout each business day. Current automation blocker: anti-bot challenge.

cadence: Daily, morning
notes: Best early-warning Minneapolis page for new applicant detail and pending actions before statewide systems catch up.

cadence: Monday and Thursday official cycle; practical check Monday/Friday
notes: Useful for liquor and drinking-establishment applications that have moved into the hearing process.

source_name: North Dakota Attorney General License Holders
cadence: Monday and Friday
notes: Best statewide North Dakota retail-license holder source. The AG says lists are updated regularly.

source_name: Fargo Liquor Control Board Agendas & Minutes
cadence: Before and after the third Wednesday meeting each month
notes: One of the strongest local pages in ND for applicant names, DBA, address, and transfer/new issue detail.

source_name: Grand Forks City Council Agendas & Minutes
cadence: Before and after the first and third Monday council meetings
notes: Best public feed for Grand Forks alcohol-license actions that advance to council.

cadence: Before and after the first three Tuesdays each month
notes: Best live public feed for Sioux Falls license actions. Official council meetings are on the first three Tuesdays at 6 p.m.

cadence: Before and after the first and third Monday meetings
notes: Best public feed for new Rapid City alcohol-license actions. Rapid City states council meets first and third Monday.

cadence: Weekly verification or when a Rapid City lead appears
notes: Strong local verification source. The page posts alcohol holder PDFs by class and available-license registry notices.

source_name: South Dakota DOR Liquor Retailers & Other Liquor Licenses
cadence: Reference page; check when working a South Dakota lead
notes: Important reference page because South Dakota routes ordinary retail licensing to local finance officers or county auditors.

source_name: North Dakota Tax Commissioner Alcohol Tax
cadence: Weekly or when checking manufacturers, wholesalers, and shippers
record_type: state alcohol tax and license lookup
fields_expected: license lookup by number or type
notes: Useful statewide source for manufacturer, wholesaler, and direct-shipper licensing outside the local retail track.

cadence: Before and after the second and fourth Tuesday commission meetings
notes: Important add-on because it links the current Bismarck alcohol license holders PDF and confirms commissioner approval is required.

cadence: Before and after the second and fourth Tuesday commission meetings
notes: Use alongside the alcohol permits page to catch commission approvals and supporting documents.

cadence: Reference page; use when a Grand Forks lead appears
notes: Useful for understanding what class of alcohol action is moving through city review.

cadence: Reference page; use with council agenda review
notes: Good local program page for categories, transfer information, and application flow.

source_name: Sioux Falls Liquor License Sealed Bid
cadence: Check when new licenses become available
notes: Important when new liquor licenses become available in Sioux Falls through the sealed bid process.

cadence: Reference page; pair with council and notifications
notes: Good Rochester program page for license types, requirements, and process timing.

cadence: Twice weekly or when verifying a Rochester lead
notes: Stronger than a normal info page because Rochester says you can search licenses and view all active city licenses.

cadence: Twice weekly
notes: Useful add-on because it exposes public hearings, other notices, and a licensing report section.

cadence: Before and after council meetings
notes: Best place for Rochester agenda packets and final actions.

cadence: Reference page; use with AGT commission review
notes: Useful because Duluth states applicants must appear before the AGT Commission before council approval.

cadence: Weekly or before/after commission activity
notes: Useful when active because agenda postings can expose applicant names and license actions.
